title: Cinderella
content:
Once there was a kind and gentle girl named Cinderella. She lived with her cold stepmother and two proud stepsisters. While her stepsisters wore beautiful gowns and rested all day, Cinderella was made to cook, clean, and scrub from dawn until dark.

One day, an invitation arrived from the royal palace. The king was holding a grand ball and everyone was invited! The stepsisters rushed to get their finest gowns and jewels. Cinderella longed to go too, but her stepmother said she must stay home and finish her chores.

After the stepsisters left, Cinderella sat by the cold fireplace and wept. Suddenly, a kind old woman appeared in a shimmer of golden light. "I am your fairy godmother," she said, "and you shall go to the ball!" She tapped a pumpkin and it became a golden carriage.

With more waves of her wand, the fairy godmother gave Cinderella a gown that shimmered like starlight and a dainty pair of glass slippers. "Go!" she said. "But you must leave before midnight. At midnight, the magic will end."

At the ball, everyone stared in wonder at the beautiful unknown girl. The prince danced only with Cinderella all evening. She was so joyful she nearly forgot the time. Then the clock began to strike midnight! She ran from the palace and lost one tiny glass slipper on the steps.

The prince found the slipper and declared he would marry whoever it fit. His servants traveled the entire kingdom. Every girl tried the slipper, but it fit no one. At last they came to Cinderella's house. The stepsisters pushed and squeezed, but it was no use.

Then Cinderella stepped quietly forward. The slipper slid onto her foot perfectly. She produced the matching slipper from her pocket. Her fairy godmother appeared and restored her beautiful gown. Cinderella, with her kind and forgiving heart, held no bitterness. She married the prince, and they lived in happiness and kindness for all their days.

author: Charles Perrault
